registry.description: Perverseness is the state of being deliberately crooked — morally twisted away from what is right, not by ignorance but by inclination. The Hebrew vocabulary here carries the image of something bent out of its natural shape, of the path that deviates from the straight way. Perverseness is treated as a settled condition rather than a single act: the perverse person has a habitual orientation away from what is good and true, and their speech and conduct reflect this inner twist.

terms.decision_reason: F4: parent H8419 in anchor list, section_type=primary

terms.medium_def:
1) to turn, overthrow, overturn
1a) (Qal)
1a1) to overturn, overthrow
1a2) to turn, turn about, turn over, turn around
1a3) to change, transform
1b) (Niphal)
1b1) to turn oneself, turn, turn back
1b2) to change oneself
1b3) to be perverse
1b4) to be turned, be turned over, be changed, be turned against
1b5) to be reversed
1b6) to be overturned, be overthrown
1b7) to be upturned
1c) (Hithpael)
1c1) to transform oneself
1c2) to turn this way and that, turn every way
1d) (Hophal) to turn on someone
terms.decision_reason: F5b: parent H8419 in anchor list, section_type=related_term, root NOT confirmed

quality_flag_detail.NOTE_ON_ROOT_FAMILY: Root family of H2015 ('to overturn') not confirmed from STEP data alone. Root consonants may not match the anchor cluster root.

terms.medium_def:
A location at Mt. Hermon first mentioned at Num.20.22; 
called Hor_Mount (הֹר) § the mountain on which Aaron died; situated on the eastern side of the valley of Arabah, the highest of the whole range of sandstone mountains in Edom; on the eastern side is the ancient city of Petra
